meta.title: Vancouver Island Campgrounds NOT managed by the BC provincial government
meta.scope: All of Vancouver Island. Includes private RV parks/resorts, First Nations-operated campgrounds, regional/municipal parks, and federal (Parks Canada) sites.
meta.updated: 2026-07-14 (added First Nations and regional district research pass)
meta.primary_sources: Pacific RV Ventures 'Big List of RV Campsites on Vancouver Island' (Parts One & Two); TinWis '5 Nuu-chah-nulth-owned Campgrounds' article; CRD, RDN, and Regional District of Mount Waddington websites
meta.completeness_note: This is a working v2 list, closer to complete but still NOT exhaustive. Vancouver Island likely has 100+ campgrounds in this category total; this list now captures ~50 well-documented full-hookup RV parks, 9 First Nations-operated sites, and most regional-district-run campgrounds. Still likely missing: smaller/rustic private campgrounds without full hookups, west-coast lodges with camping, and any regional sites not surfaced by these particular sources.

nanaimo_parksville_qualicum_mid_island.name: Westwood Lake RV Camping & Cabins (Westwood Lake Campground Resort)
Qualicum Bay Resort and RV Park

port_alberni_ucluelet_tofino.name: China Creek Marina & RV Park
port_alberni_ucluelet_tofino.note: Operated by Port Alberni Port Authority, which leases the land from Alberni-Clayoquot Regional District — functionally a hybrid private/regional-district site

courtenay_comox_campbell_river_north_isl.name: Seal Bay RV Park
courtenay_comox_campbell_river_north_isl.note: Adjacent to (but distinct from) Seal Bay Regional Park

courtenay_comox_campbell_river_north_isl.name: Driftwood By The Sea RV Park & Cottages
Sunny Sanctuary Campground / Port Hardy RV Resort

first_nations_operated.name: Secret Beach Campground & Kayak Launch
first_nations_operated.location: Ucluelet, BC (Toquaht Bay, 13km gravel off Hwy 4)

first_nations_operated.name: Qualicum First Nation Waterfront Campground, Cabins and RV Park
Puntledge RV Park & Campground
first_nations_operated.operator: Located in traditional territory of the K'ómoks First Nation — operating entity still needs confirmation (may be privately run)

first_nations_operated.name: Sooke Potholes (Spring Salmon Place / KWL-UCHUN Campground)
first_nations_operated.operator: Managed by CRD, operated by T'Sou-ke Nation

regional_district_municipal_parks.name: Spring Salmon Place (KWL-UCHUN) Campground, Sooke Potholes
regional_district_municipal_parks.note: duplicate cross-reference with First Nations list above

regional_district_municipal_parks.name: Kwaksistah Regional Park Campground
regional_district_municipal_parks.notes: 7 rustic sites, no services, unreliable cell service

regional_district_municipal_parks.name: Link River Regional Park Campground
regional_district_municipal_parks.notes: 23 waterfront/wooded sites, no services except accessible outhouses

regional_districts_with_no_camping_confi: Comox Valley Regional District — no regional park camping
Cowichan Valley Regional District — no regional park camping

federal_parks_canada.name: Green Point Campground, Pacific Rim National Park Reserve

needs_more_digging: This list captures the well-documented full-hookup RV parks; smaller/rustic private campgrounds are likely underrepresented
Puntledge RV Park operator status — confirm if First Nations-operated or privately run on traditional territory
West coast (Ahousaht, Ehattesaht, Kyuquot, Zeballos area) may have additional First Nations or lodge-based campgrounds not yet surfaced
Smaller/informal private campgrounds and farm-stay/Hipcamp-style listings not systematically catalogued — Hipcamp/RVezy searches would surface many more individual small operators
Regional District of Mount Waddington's third campground (on Malcolm Island) excluded as not on Vancouver Island proper — worth double-checking there isn't a second VI site missed
Full exhaustive coverage of this category (likely 100+ total sites) would benefit from a dedicated Research pass
